{"chapters":[{"chapter_id":"planning","heading":"Planning and Task Decomposition","body_markdown":"# Planning and Task Decomposition

Frontier LLMs have demonstrated significant improvements in planning by
decomposing complex goals into executable sub-tasks. This chapter surveys
the planning landscape as of 2025.

<!-- FIGURE: planning-loop diagram showing task decomposition cycle -->

<!-- TABLE: benchmark comparison of planning methods across frontier models -->

<!-- EQUATION: objective function for hierarchical task decomposition -->

<!-- CITATION: correa2025planningperformance -->

<!-- CITATION: wei2026agenticreasoning -->
"},{"chapter_id":"memory","heading":"Memory and State Persistence","body_markdown":"# Memory and State Persistence

Long-horizon agentic tasks require persistent memory across steps.
Lightweight cognitive architectures have emerged to address this challenge.

<!-- FIGURE: memory architecture overview comparing episodic and semantic stores -->

<!-- TABLE: memory system comparison on efficiency and recall metrics -->

<!-- EQUATION: retrieval scoring function for memory access -->

<!-- CITATION: huang2025licomemory -->

<!-- CITATION: chen2025telemem -->
